title: Research Program for Literacy Without Sound
subtitle: Study design, materials, corpora, and the next empirical steps for signed-language literacy
summary: A SignWriting-centered research program can begin with early studies, materials work, corpora, and reusable research infrastructure.
synopsis: The pathway model and reading-signed-text agenda become a practical research program organized around three areas: reading and literacy studies, Brazil-grounded empirical work, and corpus, materials, and reusable research infrastructure. The emphasis is on realistic early studies, durable research assets, research ethics, external-validity limits, and disciplined evidence-building rather than script-comparison contests or premature large-scale claims.

slides.title: Why This Program
slides.summary: The pathway model and reading agenda need practical studies, materials, corpora, ethics, and reusable infrastructure.

image_prompts.concrete: Research program slide showing pathway model and reading questions feeding into studies, materials, corpora, ethics, and infrastructure
image_prompts.abstract: A research agenda turning into durable study infrastructure

slides.speech: The pathway model and reading-signed-text agenda become a practical research program when they identify what can be studied first, what materials are needed, and what infrastructure would make future work cumulative.
slides.notes: Frame as realistic phase-one research planning.

slides.title: Three Research Areas
slides.summary: The program begins with reading and literacy studies, Brazil-grounded empirical work, and corpus, materials, and reusable research infrastructure.

image_prompts.concrete: Three-column research program slide: reading and literacy, Brazil-grounded empirical work, corpus and reusable infrastructure
image_prompts.abstract: Three research areas connected by shared materials

slides.speech: The program has three first research areas. One focuses on reading and literacy. One focuses on Brazil-grounded empirical work. One focuses on corpus, materials, and reusable research infrastructure.

slides.title: Realistic Early Studies
slides.summary: Phase-one work should include institutional mapping, materials audits, reader-experience studies, short-text pilots, and corpus-building pilots.

image_prompts.concrete: Phase-one research checklist slide with institutional mapping, materials audit, reader experience, short-text tasks, and corpus pilot
image_prompts.abstract: Small practical studies forming a first research base

slides.speech: The first studies should be realistic. Institutional mapping, materials audits, reader-experience interviews, short-text task pilots, and corpus-building pilots can produce useful knowledge without pretending to settle every outcome question.
slides.notes: Keep the scale modest and credible.

slides.title: Ethics And Infrastructure
slides.summary: The program depends on community involvement, accessible consent, data rights, governance, and infrastructure that can preserve reusable materials.

image_prompts.concrete: Research governance slide showing community involvement, accessible consent, data rights, ethics review, and reusable infrastructure
image_prompts.abstract: Ethics and infrastructure supporting a research program

slides.speech: The program is not only about study design. It also depends on ethics, community governance, accessible consent, rights and reuse, and infrastructure that can preserve materials for future research.
slides.notes: Close by linking research to infrastructure.

slides.title: Phase-One Deliverables
slides.summary: Phase one should produce durable research assets: an institutional map, materials set, pilot corpus, early reader findings, tested tasks, and ethics-ready study design.

image_prompts.concrete: Phase-one research deliverables checklist slide with institutional map, curated materials set, pilot corpus, reader-experience findings, short-text tasks, and ethics-ready study design
image_prompts.abstract: Practical research assets becoming a stronger foundation for later studies

slides.speech: Phase one succeeds if it produces durable research assets. The practical outputs are an updated institutional map, a curated materials set, a reusable pilot corpus, early reader-experience findings, tested short-text tasks, and a stronger basis for ethics-ready study design.
slides.notes: End on concrete deliverables. This keeps the program modest, fundable, and cumulative.
